title: Quality Gate
prompt: Create a release quality gate for a WordPress plugin. Include syntax checks, coding standards, Plugin Check, unit/integration tests, manual smoke tests, security review, and documentation review.
hints: A good gate is runnable by someone else, not just aspirational.

model_answer.summary: A release quality gate should be scripted, repeatable, and broad enough to catch syntax, standards, behavior, security, documentation, and schema issues.
model_answer.work: Run php -l across plugin PHP files, PHPCS with WordPress Coding Standards, Plugin Check, PHPUnit unit and integration tests, and a manual smoke pass in a disposable WordPress site. Include a security review for capabilities, nonces, permission_callback logic, validation, sanitization, escaping, and secret handling. Include i18n and accessibility checks for admin screens and blocks. Validate README accuracy, JSON course packs or block metadata, and schema files so published docs match the actual plugin behavior.
checks: PHPCS with WordPress Coding Standards passes or has documented intentional exceptions.
Manual smoke tests cover activation, deactivation, primary workflows, and failure states.
Security, i18n, accessibility, README, JSON, and schema reviews are complete.
model_answer.why_this_is_good: It is both machine-runnable and reviewer-friendly, which makes the release gate repeatable instead of ceremonial.
common_misses: Listing tools without saying what they verify.
Treating README and schema validation as optional.
model_answer.revision_prompt: Revise by turning the release gate into a runnable checklist that includes syntax, PHPCS, Plugin Check, PHPUnit, manual smoke, security, i18n, accessibility, README, JSON, and schema checks.

summary.description: One or two sentences explaining the answer.

work.description: The main implementation, review, or design answer.

checks.description: Verification steps, review checks, or acceptance criteria.
